thinking.text:
The user has provided their date of birth (11/03/1990) and phone number (312-555-0481). I need to verify their identity by looking up their information. I can use either the date of birth or phone number to search, but I need to find a way to look them up.

Looking at my available tools, I have:
- get_user_information_by_id - requires user_id
- get_user_information_by_name - requires customer_name
- get_user_information_by_email - requires email

None of these tools allow me to search by date of birth or phone number directly. I need to ask the user for either their name, email, or user ID so I can look them up in the system.

Wait, let me re-read the policy. It says I need to verify their identity by confirming 2 out of 4 fields (date of birth, email, phone number, address). But to look them up, I need to use one of the available tools which require name, email, or user_id.

So I should ask for their name or email to look them up, and then I can verify the other details they've provided.

worker_attempts.visible_answer: Thank you for providing that information! To look up your account, I'll need either your **name** or **email address**. Once I can locate your account, I'll verify the details you've provided (date of birth and phone number) to complete the identity verification.
